name: Isabella Reyes
lifeSignals: Traveling solo — usually travels with Camille
emotionalContext: May be resetting after a significant life shift. Don't perform care — let her lead. Create conditions for stillness, not stimulation. If she wants connection she will ask.

decisionTable.why: Acknowledges her return without demanding engagement
Known preference; silent gesture of recognition
High preference match; let her choose without pressure
She prefers quiet discovery; unsolicited suggestions may feel intrusive

decisionTable.opportunity: Ask about Camille or inquire about solo travel
decisionTable.why: Life signal suggests possible loss or transition — never probe

whisperBriefText: Isabella is back after 14 months — longer than usual. She's solo this time. She's an interior designer with a quiet, perceptive eye and she will notice everything. Don't over-perform. Place jasmine tea in her room before arrival. If she wants conversation, she'll start it. Your job is to make the space feel like it was waiting for her.
afterglowText: Dear Isabella. During your stay, you found the creek trail twice. You ordered jasmine tea both mornings, and you spent four quiet hours in the ceramics studio on Saturday afternoon. We noticed. We thought you might want to know — Mara Solis, the artist in residence here last weekend, has a show opening at Rosewood London in September. We thought of you immediately. Until next time. Rosewood Sand Hill.

name: Daniel Park
lifeSignals: All orders placed via app — no phone or front desk contact
emotionalContext: Private by nature, not by mood. He isn't cold — he's intentional. First-timer building his own Rosewood impression. Today is day one of his Echo profile. Let him define the relationship.

decisionTable.why: Acknowledges arrival without expectation of response
Respecting stated preference builds trust faster than re-asking
No signal he wants curation; unsolicited suggestions may feel like surveillance

decisionTable.opportunity: Follow up on app order satisfaction
decisionTable.why: He will reach out if something is wrong; checking in may feel like monitoring

whisperBriefText: Daniel is a first-timer and he's running his own experiment on us. He's watching how we treat someone who doesn't perform gratitude. Don't chase engagement. Fulfill his app orders fast and flawlessly. If he ever does reach out directly, that moment matters — treat it like a signal, not a transaction.
